{{Infobox book
| name = Soulsmith
| slug = cradle-02-soulsmith
| precedes = [[Blackflame (novel)|Blackflame]]
| follows = [[Unsouled (novel)|Unsouled]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-cradle-02-soulsmith.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2023
| isbn = 9781959001812
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The story begins with [[Yerin]], a disciple of the [[Sword Sage]], undergoing a brutal training exercise involving thirteen razor-edged swords. Through sheer willpower and [[madra]] control, she manages to fulfill her master's cryptic instructions by forcing the blades away. Meanwhile, in the [[Desolate Wilds]], [[Lindon]] follows [[Yerin]]'s advice to compress his [[madra]] core, successfully reaching the [[Copper]] realm. However, his breakthrough is interrupted when [[Yerin]] vanishes and a mysterious attacker strikes their campsite. After a violent ambush by [[Lowgold]] [[bandits]], where [[Yerin]] kills the final attacker before collapsing from her wounds, [[Lindon]] carries her away on his [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]], pursued by [[Remnants]]. They eventually find refuge in a hollow tree, only to witness a massive surge of [[vital aura]] that causes local monsters and [[sacred beasts]] to flee toward its source, prompting [[Yerin]] to investigate. 

As the [[Abidan]] [[Judge Suriel]] purges the corrupted population of the planet [[Harrow]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] encounter [[sacred artists]] at the base of the newly emerged [[Transcendent Ruins]]. They are eventually escorted to the [[Inn of the Drifting Light]] by [[Jai Sen]], though [[Yerin]] suspects his hospitality is a trap. Amidst these tensions, the disgraced prodigy [[Jai Long]] works for the [[Sandviper sect]] to map the [[Ruins]] in search of a legendary spear. Conflict escalates between the Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper and Fisher factions, drawing the attention of the powerful [[Arelius family]]. During this chaos, [[Lindon]] seeks out the [[Soulsmith]] Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha, proving his worth by repairing one of her spider-construct{{!}}spider constructs and becoming a worker in her foundry. While [[Lindon]] learns the intricacies of 'binding{{!}}bindings' and successfully forges his first crystalline blue scale, he is kidnapped by the [[Sandviper sect]]. He is eventually rescued by Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha, who directs him to focus on [[Forging]] to reach the [[Iron stage]].

Attempts by [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] to infiltrate the Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper camp result in disaster; [[Yerin]] is captured and wounded by a [[Remnant]], while [[Lindon]] is left helpless. The situation is complicated by the arrival of [[Eithan]], a powerful observer who deliberately enters Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper custody to stir chaos. [[Eithan]] eventually guides [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] through a hidden passage, revealing he is from the [[Blackflame Empire]]. After manipulating [[Yerin]] into training with him, [[Eithan]] leaves [[Lindon]] to face a centipede [[Remnant]] alone in the [[Ruins]]. Following two weeks of isolation, [[Lindon]] is rescued but must undergo the grueling 'Bloodforged' training—using [[Sandviper venom]] to burn his channels—to prepare for advancement. This process nearly kills him, forcing [[Eithan]] to trigger a violent, premature transformation to the [[Iron stage]]. 

Now an [[Iron]]-rank cultivator, [[Lindon]], [[Yerin]], and [[Eithan]] battle through waves of [[Remnants]] to reach a massive stone door in the [[Ruins]]. Behind it, they discover an ancient Soulsmith foundry{{!}}Soulsmith's foundry containing a powerful [[madra]] spear and research on specialized binding{{!}}bindings. Their discovery triggers a violent confrontation with the Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper and [[Jai clan]] members. To resolve the standoff, [[Eithan]] reveals his status as an [[Underlord]], intimidating the clan elders and negotiating a deal: [[Lindon]] is granted one year of training before facing [[Jai Long]] in a duel. The story concludes with [[Eithan]] inviting both [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] to join him, revealing his ambitious goal of finding companions to walk with him through the isolation of the [[sacred arts]].}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|A young disciple named [[Yerin]] is left alone in the snow by her master, the [[Sword Sage]], as part of a brutal training exercise involving thirteen razor-edged swords. Despite her exhaustion and bleeding wounds, she attempts to use her [[madra]] to find a weapon or escape the circle of blades. Ultimately, she manages to channel her power to make the swords slide away, fulfilling her master's cryptic instruction.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|While camping in the [[Desolate Wilds]], [[Lindon]] follows [[Yerin]]'s advice to advance his cultivation by manually compressing his [[madra]] core. He successfully reaches the [[Copper]] realm, gaining the ability to perceive [[vital aura]] in the world around him. However, his breakthrough is interrupted when he wakes up to find that [[Yerin]] has vanished and a mysterious attacker has struck the campsite.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|While protecting [[Lindon]], [[Yerin]] is ambushed by a group of [[Lowgold]] [[bandits]] and engages in a brutal fight against multiple attackers. [[Lindon]] attempts to intervene by deploying a [[White Fox boundary formation]], but [[Yerin]] ultimately kills the final spearman with a powerful [[sword]] technique before collapsing from her wounds. As [[Lindon]] carries the injured [[Yerin]] away on his [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]], they are pursued by two newly emerged [[Remnants]].}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|After escaping [[Remnants]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] take refuge in a hollow tree protected by a warding circle, only to find themselves surrounded by diseased [[sacred beasts]] and an approaching army of monsters. A sudden, massive surge of [[vital aura]] drains the environment, causing all the creatures to flee toward the source of the energy. Seizing the opportunity, [[Yerin]] uses the [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]] to fly toward the [[aura]]'s origin to investigate what caused the phenomenon.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Suriel]], an [[Abidan]] [[Judge]], uses her [[Razor]] to purge the corrupted human population of the planet [[Harrow]], leaving only a small fraction of survivors. After a tense exchange with [[Gadrael]] regarding her autonomy and the search for the missing Ozriel, she prepares to rescue the remaining untainted lives. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]], exhausted and dehydrated, encounter a group of [[sacred artists]] at the base of the newly emerged [[Transcendent Ruins]], where [[Yerin]] asserts her dominance through a series of physical confrontations.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Eithan]], a powerful observer using webs of [[madra]], identifies [[Yerin]] as the disciple of a deceased [[Sage]] and becomes intrigued by [[Lindon]] after spotting a mysterious, high-level forged [[madra]] bead in his pocket. Meanwhile, [[Jai Sen]] escorts [[Yerin]] and [[Lindon]] to the [[Inn of the Drifting Light]], sharing legends about a legendary spear hidden within the [[Transcendent Ruins]]. However, [[Yerin]] realizes [[Jai Sen]]'s hospitality is a trap designed to test or exploit them, prompting her to plan an escape through their room's window.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Jai Long]], a disgraced former prodigy of the [[Jai clan]] serving the [[Sandviper sect]], works diligently to map the [[Transcendent Ruins]] in pursuit of a legendary spear. While managing sect affairs and responding to a provocation by a mysterious [[Copper]], he is thrown into a panic by rumors that the powerful [[Arelius family]] is arriving. The chapter concludes with a background report detailing how [[Jai Long]]'s physical disfigurement and accidental absorption of a twisted [[Remnant]] led to his exile from his family.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|After negotiating for a bathhouse using [[halfsilver]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] discuss the difficulties of learning new [[Paths]] while relaxing in a hot spring. Their conversation is interrupted by an attendant warning them of impending conflict between the Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper and Fisher factions. [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] witness a confrontation involving [[Jai Long]], Young Chief [[Kral]], and the [[Fishers]], which escalates into violence after a warning about the approaching [[Arelius family]] is rejected.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|After a confrontation between the Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper and Fisher sects is interrupted by the arrival of the [[Soulsmith]] [[Fisher Gesha]] and her spider-construct{{!}}spider constructs, [[Lindon]] decides to seek out the old woman as a potential mentor. While [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] follow Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha, [[Eithan]] follows the [[Sandvipers]] and provocatively challenges them to a fight. [[Eithan]] effortlessly maneuvers through their coordinated attacks without taking damage, eventually departing after mocking the sect's honor.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Lindon]] approaches the [[Soulsmith]] Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha to offer his services, eventually proving his worth by successfully repairing one of her malfunctioning spider-construct{{!}}spider constructs. Impressed by his skill despite his low rank, Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha agrees to take him on as a worker in her foundry. Shortly after, [[Yerin]] arrives at the camp with a valuable [[Remnant]] corpse and Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper furs to negotiate shelter for herself and proper training for [[Lindon]].}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|While working as a laborer for the [[Soulsmith]] Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha, [[Lindon]] learns about 'binding{{!}}bindings'—the essential cores of constructs—and observes how they are used to imbue weapons with elemental properties. Meanwhile, at the Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper camp, the [[Highgold]] [[Jai Long]] asserts his dominance over rebellious [[miners]] by winning a duel against a prisoner while wearing a restrictive collar. Back at the foundry, [[Lindon]] applies his newfound knowledge of [[madra]] control to successfully Forge his first crystalline blue scale.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|After being kidnapped in his sleep by the [[Sandviper sect]], [[Lindon]] is imprisoned in a cage alongside several wounded [[miners]] near the [[Transcendent Ruins]]. [[Fisher Gesha]] arrives to confront the [[Sandvipers]] and successfully reclaims [[Lindon]], bringing him back to her territory. Upon their return, Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha instructs [[Lindon]] to focus on his [[Forging]] training as a means toward eventually reaching the [[Iron stage]].}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] attempt to infiltrate the Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper camp in disguise to free prisoners and steal [[dragon]] scales, but they are quickly ambushed after being sensed by the Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper guards. [[Yerin]] is captured and wounded by a [[Remnant]], while [[Lindon]] is rendered helpless as [[Fisher Gesha]] refuses to intervene. The situation shifts when [[Eithan]] arrives and deliberately surrenders himself into Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper custody, seemingly to stir further chaos.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|While working as a prisoner in the mines, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] are joined by a mysterious and powerful man named [[Eithan]] during a sudden attack by [[dreadbeasts]]. [[Eithan]] uses his superior awareness to guide them to safety through a hidden passage, revealing he is from the [[Blackflame Empire]]. After explaining his background and offering to help [[Lindon]] improve his breathing technique, [[Eithan]] sends [[Lindon]] ahead into a dark room to face a centipede [[Remnant]] alone.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|In the [[Ruins]], [[Eithan]] manipulates [[Yerin]] into joining him by using [[Lindon]] as leverage, eventually leaving the two of them to train while [[Lindon]] fights for survival against [[sacred beasts]]. Meanwhile, in the Desolate Wilds{{!}}Wilds, [[Jai Long]] of the [[Sandviper sect]] enters a tense negotiation with [[Ragahn]] of the [[Fisher sect]] over a map of the [[Ruins]]. The confrontation culminates in a duel between [[Kral]] and [[Lokk]], which the Fisher disciple wins through clever use of his [[madra]], resulting in a temporary truce between the two sects.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|After two weeks of isolation and injury, [[Lindon]] is rescued by [[Eithan]] and [[Yerin]] as the door to his chamber finally opens. To prepare for his advancement to [[Iron]] under extreme time constraints, [[Lindon]] undergoes the grueling 'Bloodforged' training by repeatedly allowing Sandviper venom{{!}}sandviper venom to burn channels into his body. The process nearly kills him, forcing [[Eithan]] to trigger a premature and violent transformation to [[Iron]] to save his life.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|As the [[Sandviper sect]] prepares for a ritual to advance a young boy to [[Iron]], [[Jai Long]] and [[Kral]] realize the [[Arelius family]] has arrived at the [[Ruins]] ahead of schedule. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] awakens to find he has successfully transitioned to an [[Iron body]], though he must deal with the physical aftermath and a lack of clothing. Alongside [[Yerin]] and [[Eithan]], [[Lindon]] battles through waves of [[Remnants]] and [[dreadbeasts]] on a spiraling staircase, eventually reaching a massive stone door carved with four [[sacred beasts]].}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|After [[Yerin]] breaks through a sealed door, [[Lindon]], [[Eithan]], and [[Yerin]] discover an ancient Soulsmith foundry{{!}}Soulsmith's foundry containing a powerful [[madra]] spear and research on specialized binding{{!}}bindings. Their discovery is interrupted by the arrival of Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper and [[Jai clan]] members, leading to a violent confrontation. During the skirmish, [[Lindon]] uses a stolen [[binding]] to wound the [[Sandviper heir]], [[Kral]], while [[Eithan]] distracts the attackers, leaving the group surrounded by hostile forces.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|After [[Jai Long]] threatens to kill [[Lindon]] for the death of his friend, Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius intervenes by revealing his status as an [[Underlord]] and using overwhelming pressure to intimidate the [[Jai clan]] elders and Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper leaders. [[Eithan]] negotiates a deal where [[Lindon]] is granted one year of training before facing [[Jai Long]] in a duel, while also securing treasures for himself and his companions. The chapter concludes with a series of probabilistic reports analyzing the potential future outcomes of the impending conflict between [[Jai Long]] and [[Lindon]].}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Yerin]] wakes up in a Fisher cottage to find she has been healed and that [[Lindon]] has advanced to the rank of [[Iron]]. [[Lindon]] explains that Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius, an [[Underlord]], has invited them both to join him, revealing that [[Eithan]] intends to recruit them as a pair. [[Eithan]] shares his ambitious goal of finding companions to walk with him to the end of the [[sacred arts]], despite the inherent isolation caused by advancing in power.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Lindon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Yerin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Jai Long]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Eithan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Fisher Gesha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Kral]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Ragahn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[sandviper]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Jai Sen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sage of the Endless Sword]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Lokk]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Gokren]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Suriel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Yerin's master]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Resh]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|old woman}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Tash]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Jai clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Fishers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Five Factions Alliance]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Ruins]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Desolate Wilds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Arelius family]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Blackflame Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandviper sect]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Wei clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Transcendent Ruins]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[sacred artists]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Purelake]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Heaven’s Glory School]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Mount Samara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Deep Eye School]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Goldsign]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Forged madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[vital aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Heart of Twin Stars]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[sword madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Bloodforged Iron body]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Ruler technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[sword aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Endless Sword]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandviper madra]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnant]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Thousand-Mile Cloud]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[parasite ring]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sylvan Riverseed]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|spider-construct}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[drudge]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[halfsilver]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Suriel's Razor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Yerin's Goldsign]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Iron]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Copper]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Gold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Highgold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[dreadbeasts]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Underlord]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Lowgold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[sacred arts]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Truegold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Soulsmith]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Iron body]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Path of Twin Stars]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[sacred artist]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|scales}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Path of the Stellar Spear]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Unsouled]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Jade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Striker]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Lindon's core]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Path]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Vital aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[dead matter]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Soulsmiths]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cradle
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL44611910W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
